Empires & Puzzles Costumed Liu Bei Review: The Warlord Reborn

The Warlord of Shu is back from retirement, and he's hit the gym. Costumed Liu Bei brings massive minion upgrades and modernized stats that turn his tactical counterplay into a brute force strategy. Is he now a top-tier threat?
Costumed Liu Bei: The Unstoppable Forest Commander
Costumed Liu Bei: The Warlord Reborn
If the original Liu Bei was a brilliant strategist let down by his aging army,
is that same strategist leading a legion of super-soldiers. Released to revitalize the War of Three Kingdoms roster, this costume transforms him from a fragile relic into a
class juggernaut. With beefed-up stats and minions that actually survive a stiff breeze, he reclaims his spot as a premier defensive and offensive support hero.

The costume trades the regal, courtly robes for hardened battle armor infused with nature's power. Vines wrap around his gear, and his stance is more aggressive. It visually communicates exactly what the stats do: he’s tougher, stronger, and ready for the front lines. The "Forest Command" theme is fitting for a Nature hero who now feels like an immovable object.
The difference is night and day compared to the base version.
A massive jump from the base 815. While not a pure sniper's stat (modern hitters push 1300+), it allows his 350% hit to actually sting.
This is where he shines. Breaking 2000 HP (base) and 2500+ with Limit Breaks puts him in the "Modern Tanky" category. He can take a hit, ensuring he summons his minions.
He sits comfortably in the modern meta, competitive with recent releases.
"Forest Command" is a direct upgrade that fixes the core issues of the original skill.
A slight bump from 340%, but combined with his much higher attack stat, the output is significantly higher. He can finish off weakened foes.
This is huge. Base Liu Bei had 16%. These minions are

Because they have so much HP, they don't die to stray hits.

is vastly superior. Since the minions live longer, the counterattack stays active longer, dealing far more total damage over the course of a match.

. That is a substantial meat shield—effectively giving the whole team 825 "overheal".
Hitting 3 enemies for 350% with ~1400 Attack (LB2) is a serious threat, not just support damage.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

In a Kingdom stack, summoning these minions grants a burst of Mana and Health. Since he summons 5 minions at once, this triggers the bonus 5 times (depending on capping rules, usually capped per skill cast or per minion, but Kingdom bonus is generally per minion summon event). It's a fantastic sustain engine.
He laughs at Grimsteel, Eloise, and Ogima (to an extent). While they might deal damage, they often cannot
the minion easily if this passive blocks the specific removal effect type, or at least he ensures he
them. (Note: Check specific interactions, usually "Unstoppable" means the minion owner is immune to effects that block minion summoning, and sometimes the minions themselves are harder to remove).
For 6 turns, he ignores defense down, silence, and mana block. This guarantees he fires his skill, which is critical for an Average speed hero.
The base hero fell off due to stat creep. The costume was the lifeline he desperately needed.
He stands tall. 33% HP minions are among the beefiest in the game (rivalling M&M and other top summoners). He isn't just "viable"; he is a strong contender.
He is excellent in Wars (especially Rush and Equalizer), Tournaments, and standard Raiding.
16% HP minions that die instantly. 60% counterattack that lasts 1 turn.
33% HP minions that survive. 55% counterattack that lasts 4-5 turns.
The massive stat boost from the costume bonus alone makes the base hero
better if you use the costume to buff the base, but why would you? The Costume skill is superior in every way.
Monk (Costume). This is an upgrade over Druid for survivability (Withstand helps resist ailments).
Maximizes his HP, which directly increases Minion HP. A 2500 HP Liu Bei makes 825 HP minions.
. The value of giving your entire team huge meat shields far outweighs hitting slightly harder.
33% HP is elite tier. They protect against snipers and DoT.
The counterattack is tied to the minion. If the minion lives, you reflect damage.
Guarantees he gets his minions out.
Rangers or heroes like Erebus who bypass minions can still hurt the hero underneath.
Heroes that punish minion summoning (passive damage) are still annoying.
Without Rush, he needs 10 tiles (or 9 with troops). He can be killed before firing if the board is bad.
An absolute pain to kill. The minions effectively heal the team repeatedly.
The minions make your team unkillable.
Costumed Liu Bei + Arco + Queen Guowang. Thousands of HP in minions and gargoyle skin/counterattack.
C. Liu Bei + Sun Quan + Diaochan. Mana generation goes through the roof.
Still dangerous due to bonus damage to minion owners.
While he has passives, pure damage that clears minions (like a 600% hit to all) will still strip them eventually.
If you pull this costume, you have won the lottery. He will be your tank, your flank, and your map stage carry. Prioritize him immediately.

He is worth the Tonics and the Alpha Aethers. The 33% minion threshold is a significant breakpoint that allows minions to survive a hit from standard heroes. He makes "No Healer" runs viable because the minions act as a massive overheal buffer.
- A question asked by the unaware. The answer is a resounding "No."
"I pulled C Liu Bei in W3K today... Happy to have him back in the rotation."
- A veteran player realizing the King has returned.

Costumed Liu Bei is a masterclass in how to rehabilitate an aging hero. By doubling the minion HP and modernizing the stats, Small Giant has turned a "has-been" into a "must-kill." He offers protection, damage, and tactical punishment in one package. He isn't just good for a "classic" hero; he's good, period.
A must-level if you enjoy winning through attrition and counter-punishment.
Liu Bei returns with a costume that fixes his biggest flaws. Massive stats, beefy minions, and unstoppable counterattacks make him a modern powerhouse. We review the Costumed Warlord of Shu.
